CASE_TRANSFERRED TO SUPERIOR COURT, RECORD AND EXHIBITS TRANSFERRED

SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL CALENDAR OF DEC-12-2019 - SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT REVERSED. THEODORE SCHLEIFER OWES CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO MAYOR'S OFFICE AN REDUCED AMOUNT OF MONEY. THE JUDGMENT ON APPEAL IS FINAL AND REPLACES THE SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT OF NOV-04-2019. JUDGE PRO TEM MICHELLE BRANT, CLERK J. RUMSEY, NOT RECORDED. (525)

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT APPELLANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MAYOR'S OFFICE RECOVER FROM RESPONDENT SCHLEIFER, THEODORE $393.25 PRINCIPAL, $15.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $408.25, RESPONDENT SCHLEIFER ADMITS HE BLOCKED 241 ORTEGA, 241-243 ORTEGA IS A DUPLEX WITH CLEARLY SEPERATED GARAGES; NEITHER GARAGE IS MARKED "241" "243". TICKET ISSUED SHOULD HAVE INDICATED "241-243", DOWNHILL GARAGE BLOCKED BUT DID NOT; IT ERRONEOUSLY SIMPLY MARKED "243". NEITHER SIDE IN THIS DISPUTE INDICATED THEY CONTACTED THE PERSON WHO CALLED IN THE COMPLAINT. THUS BOTH PARTIES ARE AT FAULT AND SHOULD SHARE COSTS. PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND CERTIFICATE OF MAILING FILED
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF - FEE FOR SERVICE $0.00 - SERVED OCT-02-2019, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MAYOR'S OFFICE
DECLARATION OF APPEARANCE ON BEHALF OF CORPORATION PURSUANT TO CCP 116.540 (B)-(H) (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT FOR DETAILS.) FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MAYOR'S OFFICE
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF NOV-04-2019 - JUDGMENT.

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF SCHLEIFER, THEODORE RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MAYOR'S OFFICE $786.50 PRINCIPAL, $30.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $816.50 STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED
TRANSMITTAL RECEIPT FILED (PLAINTIFF & DEFENANT EXHIBITS TRANSFERRED FROM SMALL CLAIMS DIVISION)

calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Dec-12-2019 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Defendant/Respondent Theodore Schleifer the moving party.
Deputy City Attorney Ryan Stevens, (415)554-3975, for the Plaintiff/Appellant, City of San Frnacisco Mayor's Office the opposing party.

This cause comes on regularly for trial de novo. All parties and witnesses are sworn.

Theodore Schleifer, Defendant/Respondent testifies on his own behalf. The following evidence is submitted:
Exhibit 1 - Photos and narrative.
Exhibit 2 - Photos and narrative.

Attorney Ryan Stevens testifies on behalf of the Plaintiff/Appellant, City of San Frnacisco Mayor's Office.

Submits copies of the Appellant's Trial Brief and document Request Judicial Notice both filed on December 10, 2019.

At the conclusion of the case, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge Pro Tem Michelle Brant, Clerk Julie Rumsey, Not Recorded. (525)
